question.text: Which of the following expenses is NOT included in the Total Expense Ratio (TER) of a mutual fund scheme?

answer.text: Securities Transaction Tax (STT) on purchases and sales

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** TER includes investment management fees, trustee fees, audit fees, custodian fees, marketing/selling expenses (including brokerage and transaction costs), and other operational expenses. However, Securities Transaction Tax (STT) is a statutory levy on transactions and is not part of TER — it is borne by the scheme separately.
**The Trap:** Investment management fees, custodian fees, and audit fees are all components of TER. Candidates might think STT is part of TER since it affects the scheme, but STT is a government tax, not an operational expense of the scheme. Brokerage is part of TER but STT is not.
**Cross-Exam Context:** The components of TER are tested in NISM V-A. Key TER components to remember: investment management fees, trustee fees, audit fees, custodian fees, marketing/selling expenses including brokerage, and registrar & transfer agent fees. STT and GST on management fees are separate.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When clients compare fund expenses, ensure they understand that TER captures most operational costs but not all transaction-related taxes. A fund with high portfolio turnover will incur more STT (outside TER), which further reduces returns. Low-turnover funds tend to be more tax-efficient even though this advantage is not visible in TER comparisons.
